title: Wicked Wholesome Ethiopian Wheat Brownies
description: A spicy Ethiopian-inspired brownie that balances heat from berbere with the richness of dark chocolate and dashi, all in a low-calorie, crumbly form perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a 9x9 inch pan with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together whole wheat flour, unsweetened cocoa powder, baking soda, baking powder, berbere, cinnamon, and salt until well combined.
In a separate bowl, beat eggs, honey, melted coconut oil, vanilla extract, and dashi extract until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Fold in dark chocolate chips and a pinch of extra berbere for extra heat if desired.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Bake for 18-20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out with a few moist crumbs.
Remove the brownies from the oven and let them cool in the pan for 10 minutes.
Transfer the brownies onto a wire rack and cut into 12 squares.
Serve warm or at room temperature, optionally dusting with powdered sugar for a subtle finish.
